body.title: Consequence check: orphan watchdog + build-time calibration
body.result: GRANNY_ORPHAN_RESET: 0 in recent channel; p90_minutes wired in but needs 10+ DONE outcomes to calibrate (insufficient data yet — expected, not a regression). Effect: clear, monitoring continues.
body.description:
**What changed:** Granny auto-resets orphaned in_progress tickets and calibrates her watchdog timeout from build-time data.
**Predicted unintended effects:**
- False-positive reset: a slow but live CC session (e.g. L ticket running a long test suite) gets reset mid-work if it exceeds the timeout. CC then sees a 'sprint' ticket it already claimed and may conflict with Granny re-dispatching it to a second CC session.
- Calibration overfitting: if the corpus is dominated by fast S tickets, p90 underestimates real L ticket time and the calibrated timeout is too aggressive.
- ps-check miss: claude session launched via tmux with a wrapper script may not show the ticket ID in its cmdline — watchdog incorrectly sees no live session.
- Double-dispatch: watchdog resets + Granny immediately re-dispatches before the old session detects the status change; two CC sessions work the same ticket simultaneously.
**Signals to watch:** GRANNY_ORPHAN_RESET log events — frequency should be low (1-2/day max in steady state, not dozens). Any ticket appearing in done twice (two GRANNY_ACK entries) in channel log. PatternTracker calibration log line; p90 values should grow as L/XL tickets complete.
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-15.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** no automated verification — observation ticket; done when predicted effects are annotated as occurred / clear / partial and ticket is closed
